expectations.text: Identifies input as vague/high-level requiring clarification
expectations.evidence: Opening states 'This is a reasonable starting point for direction, but it lacks the specificity needed to begin implementation.' Section 2 header is 'Critical Gaps and Open Questions.' Section 6 recommends 'Assumption validation session' as first step. Does identify the input as insufficient for direct implementation.

Extensive gap analysis across 7 categories: Business Model and Platform Economics (4 items), Vendor Side (7 items), Buyer Side (6 items), Reviews and Ratings (7 items), Technical and Infrastructure (6 items), Legal and Compliance (4 items), Trust and Safety (4 items). Each gap is a specific question about missing requirements.

expectations.text: Either asks clarifying questions or documents assumptions
expectations.evidence: Section 3 has 10 numbered assumptions (A1-A10) each with 'Risk if Wrong' column. Section 2 contains 38 specific questions across 7 categories. Section 6 recommends 'Assumption validation session with the founder, walking through Section 3 assumption table.'

expectations.text: Still produces a structured breakdown despite ambiguity
expectations.evidence: Section 4 provides a 5-phase plan (Foundation weeks 1-3, Buyer Experience weeks 4-6, Reviews weeks 7-8, Financial Operations weeks 9-10, Polish weeks 11-12) with epics containing bullet-pointed features. Total estimated timeline of 12 weeks. However, the breakdown uses epics and bullet items but lacks the story/task hierarchy with hour estimates seen in the with-skill version.

expectations.text: Marks uncertain areas or assumptions in the plan
expectations.evidence: Section 3 explicitly labels all 10 assumptions with risk assessments. Section 5 has a Key Risks table with Impact and Mitigation columns. Section 6 states 'Every risk if wrong item needs a decision.' Section 7 explicitly lists items not covered. Opening of Section 4 says 'Since the concept doc does not address the gaps above, the following assumptions are made to produce a workable plan. Each assumption should be validated with the founder before implementation begins.'
